author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Public surface

- RelationshipKindRegistry and schema validation.
- Append, remove or supersede, and inspect relationship events.
- Snapshot-aware graph reader and index interfaces.
- Bounded adjacency, path, closure, impact, and subgraph queries.
- Explain and cost metadata suitable for token-aware context selection.
- Import, export, merge, and extension hooks built from the same contract.

## Non-goals

This item does not choose product-specific relevance weights, silently invent relationships, or force large indexes on scratch projects. Those belong to context-engine policy, audits, and adaptive storage layers.
SDK relationship registry and graph-query primitives: custom typed edges, adjacency, paths, closures, impact, and bounded subgraphs
Promote relationship definition, mutation, indexing, and bounded traversal into stable public SDK APIs so every adapter and domain model shares one implementation.

The registry defines built-in and extension-provided kinds with direction, inverse, order-bearing and hierarchy flags, cardinality, payload schema, lifecycle, aliases, and compatibility version; Mutation APIs validate endpoints and invariants then append attributable immutable events without direct state rewrites; Read APIs cover adjacency, predecessors, successors, ancestors, descendants, shortest and constrained paths, transitive closure, reverse impact, and induced bounded subgraphs; Every query supports deterministic ordering, typed filters, field projection, cursor or streaming continuation, cancellation, snapshot selection, and explicit cost or truncation metadata; Indexes are rebuildable from history, incrementally maintained, crash safe, observable, and optional for small projects; Thin CLI and MCP adapters call public SDK APIs without hidden core imports; shell-safe JSON, JSONL, TOON, and counts-first outputs preserve pipeline composability; Contract, property, replay, concurrency, migration, and scale tests cover legacy kinds, custom kinds, cycles, snapshots, and one million items
Make the public SDK the complete source of truth for relationship schemas and graph operations
CLI, MCP, extensions, scripts, and non-PM applications can build on one deterministic and extensible graph contract
Removes adapter-local graph logic and unlocks typed domain relationships, scalable context assembly, and future graph algorithms
Versioned SDK APIs for kind registries, immutable mutations, adjacency and path queries, closures, impact traversal, and bounded subgraphs
The CLI has dependency operations and visualization, but custom semantic kinds and complete public graph primitives lack a canonical SDK contract

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: Evidence: delivered the first broad public graph foundation: versioned built-in/custom relationship registry, alias compatibility, immutable in-memory index construction from item metadata, deterministic adjacency/closure/reverse-impact/shortest-path/subgraph queries with cancellation and explicit cost/truncation metadata, SDK barrel/docs, and validate integration. Packed tarball consumer proved a custom company owns graph plus real CLI lifecycle. Full repository run passed 286 files/5664 tests; focused all-source coverage for relationships.ts is 100/100/100/100. Remaining event mutation, durable incremental index, snapshot/streaming, MCP projection, and million-item scale acceptance stay on this feature, so it will return to open rather than be falsely closed.

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: A storage-independent relationship registry is the semantic boundary: algorithms must consume ordering/hierarchy flags rather than infer meaning from labels, while durable event/index implementations can remain interchangeable.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation constraints recorded 2026-07-17 (review pass #96), two hard facts for whoever claims this slice. (1) src/sdk/runtime.ts gate headroom is 2 lines (census on pm-yh8r) — this slice's registry/graph-query exports CANNOT go through runtime.ts; ship the runtime partition first/with it, or route the new primitives through a dedicated subpath module (precedent: PR#571-lane sdk/test + sdk/diagnostics folders; sdk/query/ is the natural home). (2) Perf shape to fix while building the query primitives: pm deps --format context today assembles the FULL workspace RelationshipGraph + dangling-reference collection on every call (assembleDepsRelationshipGraph in src/sdk/dependencies.ts calls collectDanglingDependencyReferences + RelationshipGraph.fromItems over ALL items before bounding) — bounded output but unbounded assembly cost, O(all items) per invocation. The adjacency/bounded-subgraph primitives owned here are the natural place to make assembly incremental/indexed so bounded queries stop paying full-workspace cost; pm-m2il's scale matrix (query-budget axis) will measure it, pm-9rxu bl8x cache contract (CACHE_VERSION) is the substrate if an index is persisted.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation progress (graph-query-surface lane): new workspace-facing pm graph command with eight subcommands (ancestors/descendants/predecessors/successors/paths/impact/analyze/audit) as a thin adapter over the public SDK graph toolkit — one assembleWorkspaceRelationshipGraph call per invocation, case-insensitive root resolution excluding missing placeholders, counts-first envelopes with explicit truncated+cost metadata, --summary suppression, cursor continuation, and registry-validated --kind filters. Wired end-to-end: CLI registration, PmClient.graph + module-level graph(), MCP pm_graph narrow tool (29-tool surface), action schema/flag/option/parameter contracts, bash/zsh/fish completions, contracts fixture regen, and docs (COMMANDS.md Graph Queries, RELATIONSHIP_GRAPH.md native-adapter paragraph, SDK.md, AGENT_GUIDE.md). runtime.ts input-normalization helpers partitioned into sdk/runtime-input.ts (~450 lines freed below the file-size gate, restoring yh8r headroom).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Graph-query-surface lane continuation (2026-07-17, second tranche): pm graph grew communities/redundancy/dominators subcommands (11 total) as thin adapters over new sdk/graph/analytics.ts; hierarchyParentEndpoint/orderingPredecessorEndpoint promoted to module exports so analytics reuses traversal's semantic-orientation logic (single implementation, no clones). runtime.ts lost two now-unused runtime-input imports (isRecord, updateManyUpdateOptionsFromFlat) flagged by eslint after the partition. Test-lane fix: mcp-dynamic-package-actions impact assertion assumed pm_deps options.dep mutates — it never does (read projection); edge now created via pm_update, and the silent nested-options drop is filed as pm-upi0.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Graph-query-surface tranche verification (commit a3b47c97): 301 test files / 5,842 tests pass at exact 100/100/100/100 coverage; eslint, jscpd zero-clone, 100% docstring, package-boundary, token-budget, and typecheck gates pass. pm graph now spans 11 subcommands; manual temp-workspace acceptance covered CLI (release DAG: dominators ranked the gating publish item, redundancy flagged the shortcut blocked_by edge with witness), PmClient.graph, and raw SDK analytics on a custom commits_to VCS registry (c1->c3 transitive shortcut detected, dominator tree correct). Coverage hardening added run-defensive.spec (non-TypeError kernel failures rethrown unchanged) and empty-workspace zero-value envelopes.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Pre-push Greptile review 009b6487-99a0-489e-9bbb-c3ee280a0d66 (confidence 3/5, three findings, all fixed): (P1) pm_graph MCP subcommand enum was hardcoded at 8 values so communities/redundancy/dominators were unreachable over MCP transport — GRAPH_SUBCOMMAND_VALUES moved into sdk/cli-contracts/enum-contracts.ts as the single source (tool-definitions stays dependency-light since it already imports that module), run.ts re-exports for barrel compatibility, contracts fixture regenerated (the stale enum had propagated into full.json), and a handshake parity test now locks the schema enum to the shared contract. (P1) GraphCommunitiesResult gained the explicit truncated flag every sibling envelope carries (community_count vs sample bound). (P2) O(k^2) linear dedup scans replaced with Sets in collectTransitiveFamilies (per-family adjacency now Set-built, sorted once per scan) and indexReachableSubgraph. 95 affected tests pass; lint/static/boundary/token gates re-verified.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #580 MERGED 2026-07-17T21:30Z (squash 6b8ee9e4): workspace graph query surface shipped — pm graph (11 subcommands), pm_graph MCP tool with contract-locked enum, PmClient.graph/graph() typed with GraphSubcommand, runtime-input partition. Review rounds: pre-push Greptile 009b6487 (3 findings fixed: MCP enum gap via shared GRAPH_SUBCOMMAND_VALUES in enum-contracts, communities truncated field, Set-based dedup) then 9676b797 5/5 clean; PR round 1 Greptile P1 (convergence truncation) + 6 CodeRabbit findings (dominators maxDepth bound, per-node abort, GraphSubcommand typing, after-description scoping, two test-strength fixes) all fixed in 0fcd2044 and confirmed by CodeRabbit; afbc9d49 regenerated the contracts fixture from a fresh build (regen-against-stale-dist trap). All 30 checks green. Remaining canonical scope unchanged: durable incremental indexes, migration/backfill, stream projections, crash recovery, million-item validation (pm-m2il matrix).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Sequencing constraint RELAXED (2026-07-17): the runtime.ts headroom-2 constraint cross-noted here and on pm-yh8r no longer binds — PR#580 extracted runtime-input.ts, runtime.ts now 3036/3400 gate-LOC (~364 headroom). ju83 remainder work (durable storage adapters, CLI/MCP graph query integration, migration/backfill, million-item validation) no longer requires a prior runtime partition. New gate-frontier files are cli/main.ts (15 headroom) and sdk/query/search.ts (29) — relevant only if ju83 wiring touches main.ts registration paths; prefer registering via register-list-query.ts/graph.ts shims as PR#580 did (graph.ts is a 7-line shim).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Storage-adapter remainder design input (2026-07-17): external package unbraind/pm-graph (2026.7.15, neo4j-driver ^6.2.0, commands namespaced `pm-graph ping/export/cypher/sync/status/query/neighbors`) is the existing durable-graph-store exemplar — no command collision with native `pm graph` (verified). The adapter interface this item ships should let that package become a conforming backend (sync/export path) rather than a parallel model; related pm-7n5a owns the external Neo4j config surface.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation progress (graph-platform lane 2026-07-18): the O(all-items)-per-invocation assembly cost recorded in the 2026-07-17 constraint note is now amortized — deps --format context and all pm graph subcommands resolve assembly through the shared fingerprint-keyed WorkspaceGraphCache (sdk/graph/cache.ts), so bounded queries in long-lived hosts (MCP server, SDK consumers) pay full-workspace normalization once per workspace change instead of once per call. Cross-surface reuse proven by test: a deps context call populates the cache and a subsequent pm graph audit reports assembly:hit. Remaining canonical scope unchanged: durable on-disk incremental indexes, migration/backfill, stream projections, deeper crash recovery, million-item validation (pm-m2il matrix).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR#581 MERGED 2026-07-18 (second ju83 tranche): WorkspaceGraphCache (src/sdk/graph/cache.ts) — SHA-256 relationship-fingerprint keying, structured-clone isolated memoization, bounded LRU across workspaces, shared by all pm graph subcommands + pm deps --format context; resolves the recorded O(all-items) per-query deps-context assembly cost; cache{fingerprint,assembly,result} observability on every graph envelope. Remaining frontier unchanged in kind: durable storage adapters (pm-graph/Neo4j exemplar per note 5), rebuildable/crash-safe persistent indexes, migration/backfill of legacy kinds, and the million-item conformance run (shared with pm-m2il).

value.text: Implementation evidence 2026-07-18: added validated extension-contributed relationship-kind registration with policy/capability attribution, declarative composition, and native workspace graph assembly; added immutable bounded stream/project folds to in-memory and durable relationship event stores; injected host-bound SDK services into extension commands. A real SDK-dispatched package command exposed and fixed activation-queue re-entry by using a symbol-scoped active-host PmClient path. Full repository coverage: 308 files, 5,892 tests, exact 100/100/100/100. Typecheck, ESLint, static/docstring/package-boundary/token/docs/contracts gates pass.

value.author: codex-sdk-vcs-20260718
value.text: Extension commands must not recursively load extensions through their injected PmClient. The outer host already owns the active schema/registry context; use PmClient.forActiveExtensionHost so nested native lifecycle actions bypass activation queue reacquisition while retaining active registrations.

Accepted: SDK action globals now carry author end-to-end; extension host client uses global author; registration validates RelationshipKindDefinition arrays directly without generic sanitizing casts; stream checkpoint boundary is explicitly documented. Focused typecheck/lint/tests pass. Review identifier will be amended from greptile status if exposed.

The public registry remains intentionally strict for required ordering, hierarchy, and allowSelf semantics, with new missing-field acceptance tests. Full sandbox coverage passed: 308 files, 5,896 tests, exact 100/100/100/100.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR#587 MERGED 2026-07-18 (third ju83-lineage tranche, SDK relationship event platform): extension-contributed relationship-kind registration/validation/composition (registerRelationshipKinds + RelationshipKindRegistry with direction, inverse, ordering, cardinality, lifecycle, aliases, version; strict contract rejects malformed containers), native workspace graph assembly now merges active package ontologies across CLI/MCP/SDK, bounded immutable RelationshipEventLog/RelationshipEventStore JSONL streams with reducer-based projections (in-memory + durable, point-in-time reads, checkpoint boundary documented), host-bound SDK injection into extension command handlers carrying caller authorship into durable events. Proven by the @unbrained/pm-vcs exemplar (pm-xtrd CLOSED). AC remainder UNCHANGED: durable storage adapters, persistent rebuildable indexes, migration/backfill, million-item validation — fingerprint still reads all items per one-shot invocation.

It confirmed the durable cache atomic-write, fingerprint invalidation, status byte accounting, CLI contracts, and SDK coverage after the review fixes.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Track test run summary (test-local-mrsplbni-wx62sg)

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Final branch evidence before PR: 318 test files / 5,993 tests passed with exact 100% statements (40,144/40,144), branches (28,184/28,184), functions (8,148/8,148), and lines (39,084/39,084). All 10 linked tests passed, including full coverage, graph SDK/CLI contracts, VCS package acceptance, and strict static gates. Fresh packed-package temp proof installed 2026.7.19, rebuilt a two-item graph index, reused a query across processes, invalidated after mutation, and imported the public SDK to model custom company owns plus commit ordering relationships.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #622 review round 1: accepted Greptile P1 registry-semantic invalidation and CodeRabbit canonical rebuild warming. Workspace fingerprints now include stable full relationship-kind definitions and both graph/deps assembly paths consume that exact registry; rebuilding with unrelated query flags still warms plain summary keys. Accepted locale-independent new sort findings; declined Gemini's process-local durable-view cache because it would violate clear/rebuild and cross-process coherence. Final review-fix coverage: 318 files / 5,994 tests, exact 100/100/100/100.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Review round 2 evidence: custom extension relationship kinds are now accepted by graph and deps context filters through the same resolved registry used for assembly/fingerprints. Deterministic governance sorting avoids JSON serialization and implicit tuple coercion. Full suite passes 5,995 tests with exact 100/100/100/100 coverage plus strict static gates.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #622 final review round: hardened the durable SDK baseline schema for array/nested-count defects and preserved compatibility for pre-duplicateRows public assemblies. Per-type baseline drift is now a first-class signed delta. Exact full coverage and strict static gates pass.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #622 durability review evidence: accepted Gemini concurrency and best-effort cache findings. Durable graph writes now acquire the shared cross-process lock for read-merge-atomic-write, so concurrent MCP/CLI completions retain both query entries; clear uses the same lock. Non-authoritative write failures no longer reject successful graph queries. Regressions cover concurrent writes and injected storage failure. Full suite: 318 files / 5,996 tests, exact 100/100/100/100 (40,184 statements; 28,239 branches; 8,154 functions; 39,121 lines); lint/static gates pass.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Final delayed-review remediation evidence: durable graph envelope decoding now rejects array-shaped results maps, invalid graph maintenance/baseline flag combinations are rejected before relationship-registry resolution, fingerprinting, or graph-cache assembly, and a spy-backed regression proves no workspace cache lookup occurs. Verification: focused durable-cache suite 10/10; pnpm lint passed (ESLint, zero jscpd clones, static quality, package-boundary, token-budget, 100% docstrings); full sandboxed coverage passed 318 files / 5,996 tests at 100% statements (40,184), branches (28,240), functions (8,154), and lines (39,121).

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #622 latest Gemini follow-up: accepted the case-folded duplicate-row ordering concern while preserving the project's locale-independent determinism rule. Duplicate governance rows now sort holder, target, and kind by lowercase canonical value with exact-spelling tie-breakers; regressions exercise case-only holder/target/kind ties. Focused governance 19/19, ESLint/static quality, zero-clone jscpd, 100% docstrings, package-boundary/token-budget, and full 318-file / 5,996-test coverage all pass at exact 100/100/100/100 (40,191 statements; 28,240 branches; 8,155 functions; 39,126 lines).

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #622 review follow-up: Gemini's two AuditNodeState findings were rejected after source verification because the interface declares id and nodeStates populates id from detail.id; no runtime defect exists. Gemini/CodeRabbit's shared legacy snapshot concern was accepted for direct public-SDK callers: per-type delta reads now tolerate pre-coverage_by_type baseline/current payloads, with regressions proving zero-based positive and negative typed deltas. Full strict verification: 318 files / 5,996 tests, exact 100% statements (40,194), branches (28,242), functions (8,156), lines (39,129); lint, zero-clone, static complexity, 100% docstrings, package boundary, and token budget pass.

value.author: codex-wide-sdk
value.text: Review-loop evidence: normalized graph item types at the SDK assembly boundary and in cache identity, preventing whitespace-only representation differences from splitting per-type governance or invalidating durable/memoized graph state. Focused graph tests passed (36/36); full repo coverage remains 100/100/100/100 across 5,996 tests.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Merged delivery evidence: PR #622 merged to main as e98282a1 with the durable cross-process graph index, extension-aware registry/cache semantics, baseline persistence, CLI/MCP/contracts/docs, and compatibility hardening. Exact-head review/check inventory was fully green and post-merge release/telemetry verification passed. This durable-index tranche is shipped; the feature returns open for migration/backfill and million-item conformance work already recorded in its metadata.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Graph/index tranche evidence: relationship graph reads now consume an effective metadata source cursor that includes the collapsed mutation delta, so supported relationship-field changes are visible to later CLI processes and long-lived SDK hosts without whole-store scans. CLI create/copy/restore import the projection contract through src/sdk/item-metadata-index.ts, satisfying the CLI-on-SDK boundary ratchet. GH-628 graph runner allocation cleanup is included. Focused graph/SDK/integration tests (205) and the exact 6071-test 100% coverage gate pass. This rolling relationship feature remains open for its broader 1M/custom-domain acceptance after this shipped tranche.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Review-loop follow-up: CodeRabbit correctly identified that the public SDK facade should document its no-op lock and warning-token contracts, and that the real-lock regression test should derive its scale fixture from DEFAULT_DERIVED_INDEX_MINIMUM_ITEMS rather than hardcode 500. Applying both plus the related graph-doc wording correction on the current PR head.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Review fixes verified: the SDK now exports and documents DEFAULT_DERIVED_INDEX_MINIMUM_ITEMS plus no-op-lock/warning behavior; the real-lock test derives 501 items from that contract; graph docs use the correct compound modifier. Focused SDK/index tests pass 132/132, pnpm lint passes with exact 100% docstrings and zero clones, typecheck passes, and docs links pass.

author: codex-root
message: Derived-index graph freshness tranche complete in PR #636; broader migration/backfill and 1M/custom-domain acceptance remain explicitly open.

value.author: codex-graph-platform-20260722
value.text: Portable graph backends should exchange a complete content-addressed snapshot containing both topology and ontology. A storage adapter then needs only read, atomic replace, optional clear, cancellation, and compare-and-swap semantics; conformance can be shared across JSON, database, and remote-service packages.

patch.value: Completed the public relationship graph platform across its full lineage: typed ontologies, immutable event history, durable migration/index/cache infrastructure, bounded graph queries and analytics, portable content-addressed snapshots, storage-neutral CAS adapters, federation, reusable adapter conformance, deterministic property fixtures, million-node acceptance, public docs, installed-package proof, and exact repository-wide 100/100/100/100 coverage.
